title: peucedanum altissimum
intro: Peucedanum altissimum, also known as tall hog's fennel or tall meadow parsley, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Peucedanum altissimum include its ability to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Peucedanum altissimum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote respiratory health and well-being.
